title: Ordered-cat specialist with purged OOF rank blending
summary: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics are designed to reduce prediction-shift leakage on sparse categories, which fits this dataset’s many rare drivers and race contexts.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and ordered categorical statistics are designed to reduce prediction-shift leakage on sparse categories, which fits this dataset’s many rare drivers and race contexts. For ROC-AUC, scikit-learn notes isotonic can change ranking via ties, and CV-stacking literature warns naive holdout stacking can create spurious AUC effects, so ensemble hygiene matters as much as the base learner.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost Ordered-mode specialist on raw high-cardinality categoricals and composites such as `Driver x Compound`, `Race x LapBucket`, `Driver x Stint`, and `Race x Compound`, under strict race-group CV. Blend specialists with the current best tabular model using purged OOF predictions plus rank averaging or sigmoid/Platt calibration only, not isotonic.
expected_effect: Improves sparse categorical interaction handling and gives a safer ensemble lift without damaging AUC rank order.
risk: This develops an already-explored direction, so gains may be incremental; strict group-aware OOF generation is mandatory.
